summary:
The permanent upper lateral incisor has a mesiodistal width larger than its cervicoincisal height; thus, it is the sole anterior tooth exhibiting such proportionality according to fundamental principles of human dentition structure.

\boxed{D}

Based solely on the provided text content from Agent2:

Despite lacking explicit supporting documentation within the retrieved studies—none addressing anterior tooth dimensional comparisons—the agent ultimately relies on well-established dental anatomic knowledge stating that the **permanent upper lateral incisor** alone displays a mesiodistal size surpassing its cervicoincisal dimension. This recognized characteristic makes it distinct among all anterior teeth, leading to confirmation of Answer Choice D.
